summary_text: Observed sequence from repo context indicates a pure startup-policy failure around 10:57–10:58, not a Notion/X API path issue: - Scheduler starts, configures logging, then runs single-writer check immediately: [`scheduler.py` main startup](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L627-L633) calls `_check_single_writer_policy()` before PID/secrets. - `_check_single_writer_policy()` resolves designated host from env or `.designated-host` and does substring comparison against `socket.gethostname()`. No fallback/override in launchd plist: [`scheduler_health.py` logic](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_health.py#L195-L229). - At 2026-04-04 10:57:45/10:57:58/10:58:15 the process logged: `SINGLE WRITER VIOLATION: scheduler running on 'Mac' but designated host is 'MacBook-Pro-M5-5.local'`. - That failure caused immediate process exit; launchd relaunched it repeatedly (KeepAlive), producing repeated violations. - At 10:58:45 startup succeeds and immediately logs secret validation and normal loop start, which matches `.designated-host` having been changed to `Mac` (`socket.gethostname()`) right before that run. Critical caveats + minimal robustness fixes (no code changes now): - Active launchd config uses direct `scheduler.py` launch and no designated-host env: [/Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist](/Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist). `ProgramArguments` bypasses `run-scheduler.sh`, so host setting is entirely file/env-sensitive. - There is a second stale/plausibly unrelated plist in repo root (`com.cursorvers.x-auto.plist`) with a different path layout; avoid drift by keeping one canonical job definition. - Host matching is brittle: `'Mac'` vs `'MacBook-Pro-M5-5.local'` fails despite being same machine naming variant. Make hostname matching resilient (canonicalize both sides, strip domains like `.local`, handle short/full FQDN variants, and compare token sets/normalized values rather than a single raw substring). - Do not let policy be effectively disabled by accident: if both env and file are missing/empty, current behavior allows bypass. Fail closed or require explicit emergency override flag only. - Add launchd hardening for fail-loop safety (`ThrottleInterval`, optional alerting after N rapid exits in Y seconds) because repeated policy exits can rapidly churn under KeepAlive. Potential fix priority: 1) canonical host comparison, 2) unify launchd launcher (`run-scheduler.sh` or explicit `X_AUTO_DESIGNATED_HOST` in plist), 3) close-policy-by-default when designated host is absent/blank, 4) remove/retire duplicate plist variants.
